grammarRuleDetail: und simply links two clauses. Verb stays in position 2 in each clause — no inversion.

usage.de: Ich lerne Deutsch und spreche Englisch.
usage.en: I learn German and speak English.

usage.de: Er kam und wir aßen zusammen.
usage.en: He came and we ate together.

grammarRuleDetail: Negates verbs and predicative adjectives. Use kein- to negate nouns with indefinite articles.

grammarRule: Verb moves to end of clause (Verbendstellung)
grammarRuleDetail: weil introduces a subordinate clause. The conjugated verb is pushed to the very end. Modal + infinitive: modal goes last.

usage.de: Ich bleibe zu Hause, weil es regnet.
usage.en: I stay home because it is raining.

usage.de: Er ist müde, weil er nicht geschlafen hat.
usage.en: He is tired because he didn't sleep.

grammarRule: No word order change — verb stays in position 2
grammarRuleDetail: Coordinating conjunction: does not occupy position 1, so no inversion occurs.

usage.de: Ich möchte kommen, aber ich habe keine Zeit.
usage.en: I would like to come, but I have no time.

usage.de: Das Wetter ist kalt, aber schön.
usage.en: The weather is cold but beautiful.

grammarRuleDetail: As a conjunctive adverb, trotzdem counts as position 1 when it starts a sentence, pushing the verb to position 2 and the subject to position 3.

usage.de: Es regnete. Trotzdem gingen wir spazieren.
usage.en: It was raining. Nevertheless we went for a walk.

usage.de: Sie war krank, trotzdem kam sie.
usage.en: She was sick, yet she came.

grammarRuleDetail: Like weil, obwohl creates a subordinate clause with verb-final order. Expresses concession — contradiction between clauses.

usage.de: Er kam, obwohl er krank war.
usage.en: He came although he was sick.

usage.de: Obwohl es teuer ist, kaufe ich es.
usage.en: Although it is expensive, I buy it.

grammarRule: Adjective: add case/gender endings before noun. Adverb: no endings.
grammarRuleDetail: Adjective endings depend on article type and case/gender/number. Superlative: am schnellsten.
structureFormula: Adverb: V + schnell | Adjective: Art. + schnell[ending] + N

usage.en: He is a fast runner. (attributive adj, m nom)

english: especially since / all the more so
grammarRuleDetail: zumal (da) creates a reinforcing subordinate reason clause with verb-final order.

usage.de: Das ist riskant, zumal wir wenig Zeit haben.
usage.en: That is risky, especially since we have little time.

usage.de: Er sollte aufpassen, zumal da er krank ist.
usage.en: He should be careful, all the more so since he is sick.

grammarRule: Verb-final — same structure as obwohl, formal register
grammarRuleDetail: Literary/formal equivalent of obwohl. Used in essays and academic writing.

usage.de: Wenngleich die Ergebnisse positiv sind, bleibt Skepsis angebracht.
usage.en: Even though the results are positive, skepticism remains appropriate.

grammarRuleDetail: Formal written connector. Summarises a preceding cause and introduces the consequence. Position 1 triggers inversion.

usage.de: Der Vertrag wurde nicht unterzeichnet. Infolgedessen scheiterten die Verhandlungen.
usage.en: The contract was not signed. As a result, the negotiations failed.

grammarRule: Position 1 → V2 inversion. Literary register only.
grammarRuleDetail: Archaic/literary equivalent of dennoch. Used in academic, legal, and literary texts only. Triggers inversion when in position 1.

usage.de: Die Beweise waren dünn. Nichtsdestoweniger wurde er verurteilt.
usage.en: The evidence was thin. Nonetheless he was convicted.
